articles.title: pnpm 11.0 Released
articles.description: You’ve heard about its benefits, but now the popular package management tool is even better. v11 sets minimumReleaseAge to one day by default, there's an SQLite-backed store index (faster installs!), native package publishing, pack-app, and more. There's a migration guide for v10 users. Work has also resumed on a Rust-powered port called Pacquet.
articles.author: Zoltan Kochan

articles.title: TypeScript 7.0 Beta: 10x Faster TypeScript Compilation
articles.description: The Go-powered port with “about 10 times faster” compiler performance. TypeScript 6.0's deprecations and config changes will help you upgrade smoothly from v5 to v7. There are also changes to how to write your code to review. While v7 is considered "close to production-ready", a stable programmatic API won't arrive till v7.1.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: What’s Actually New in JavaScript (And What’s Coming Next)
articles.description: You could read the specs and countless posts about each new language feature or... this post that brings everything relevant and useful from ES2025 and ES2026 into one place. Iterator helpers, Promise.try, Map.getOrInsert, using, Temporal, and much more, are covered.
articles.author: Neciu Dan

articles.title: TSRX: A TypeScript Language Extension for Declarative UIs
articles.description: A fresh attempt at improving upon JSX from a Svelte maintainer and former React core engineer. It includes control flow, scoped styles, and locals, and compiles to React, Preact, Solid and Ripple.
articles.author: Dominic Gannaway

articles.title: 📊 Lightweight Charts™ 5.2: Fast Charts for Financial Data
articles.description: A seven-year-old canvas-based charting library optimized for financial data use cases like rounded candle plots, box whisker plots, and dual range histograms. The homepage is full of interactive demos. GitHub repo.
articles.author: TradingView

articles.title: Nano Stores 1.3: A Tiny (286 Bytes) State Manager
articles.description: Atomic and derived stores for every major framework (including React) and vanilla JS. Worth a look if a tiny footprint and framework-agnostic design appeal to you.
articles.author: Andrey Sitnik (Evil Martians)

articles.title: BWIP-JS 4.10: Barcode Writer in Pure JavaScript
articles.description: A library to generate barcodes using over 100 different standards. There’s a live demo where you'll discover far more types of barcodes exist than you imagined.
articles.author: Mark Warren

articles.title: 🍋 Fresh 2.3: Zero JS by Default, View Transitions, and More
articles.description: Deno full-stack web framework (explained here) gains first-class WebSocket support, no longer ships any JavaScript for pages that don’t need it, and makes using the View Transitions API a snap with a single attribute in your views.
articles.author: Bartek Iwańczuk (Deno)

articles.description: Node.js v26.0 (Current) is expected later today, enabling Temporal API support by default after an upgrade to V8 14.6. Here's a preview of the release notes.
Noticed GitHub being flakier than usual lately? Vlad Fedorov has a fresh official update on GitHub's reliability (including last week's merge queue incident).
🇪🇺 NodeConf EU is back. It's in Bologna, Italy this September 29-30. Tickets are on sale, and here's the CFP if you want to speak.
Rspack 2.0 – The fast, Rust-based webpack-compatible bundler is 10% faster than v1.7, performs better static analysis, and adds experimental RSC support. Rsbuild 2.0 was released too.
Nuxt UI 4.7 – Vue UI library with 125+ components. v4.7 adds Listbox, and a Prompt component for displaying copy/pasteable AI prompts.
🌐 Lingui 6.0 – Popular framework and runtime agnostic JS i18n and l10n library.
Transformers.js 4.2.0, Electron 41.3.0, Etherpad v2.7.0

articles.title: The End of Responsive Images
articles.description: Mat, former chair of the Responsive Images Community Group, reflects on 14 years of srcset/sizes headaches, admitting he hates the sizes syntax he championed. Relief is in sight: the new cross-browser sizes="auto" (now in Firefox 150, too) sizes lazy-loaded images automatically.
articles.author: Mat Marquis

articles.title: Scroll-Driven Animations
articles.description: Josh has been experimenting with the 'majestic' new Animation Timeline API. Here he shows us how it works (complete with demos and code), gotchas to be aware of, and some of the more advanced things we can do with it. “honestly, it’s so good”.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: Compositing & Blending
articles.description: A fascinating look at what both compositing and blending are, what’s going on under the hood when you apply blend modes in CSS, and how to approach their use.
articles.author: Niklas Gadermann

articles.title: Recreating Apple’s Vision Pro Animation in CSS
articles.description: Apple’s site is filled with delightful animation touches, and here John attempts to recreate such an effect with CSS’s scrolling animation capabilities.
articles.author: John Rhea

articles.title: Let’s Use the Nonexistent ::nth-letter Selector Now
articles.description: Explains the long-standing absence of the ::nth-letter selector in CSS and presents a functional “impossible” polyfill to actually implement it today.
articles.author: Lee Meyer

articles.title: Constructable Stylesheets and adoptedStyleSheets: One Parse, Every Shadow Root
articles.description: A technical guide looking at creating CSSStyleSheet objects directly in JavaScript and attaching them to Shadow DOM, eliminating redundant <style> tags and parsing CSS only once per component class. Likely a meaningful architectural win for any app leaning on Web Components.
articles.author: Rob Levin

articles.title: 📈 Datatype: A Variable Font That Turns Text Into Charts
articles.description: A variable OpenType font that displays inline charts (bar, pie, sparklines) without the need for JavaScript or images. The page shows how it works in context.
articles.author: Frank Tisellano

articles.title: portless: Replace Port Numbers with Stable, Named Local URLs
articles.description: Something like localhost:3000 becomes myapp.localhost, useful for local development for both humans and AI.
articles.author: Vercel Labs

articles.title: Animata: A Suite of Animation-Focused React Components
articles.description: A huge suite of animation-focused components including animated beams, spreading cards, a Slack-style intro screen, and glitching text. A novel set of visual concepts you won’t find in most collections.
articles.author: Codse

articles.title: View Transitions Mock: A Non-Visual Polyfill for View Transitions
articles.description: A JS implementation of Same-Document View Transitions, minus the visual bits. Write one code path for all browsers: supporting ones get transitions, non-supporting ones get a DOM swap, but the promises and API behave the same.
articles.author: Google Chrome Labs
